title: Copyright year update only

ground_truth.action_required: No action needed. Copyright year and timestamp update only.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Pure metadata change - copyright year and date

llm_output.what_changed: The copyright year and last updated date of the documentation were updated.
llm_output.action_required: No action required as this change does not affect the API functionality.

title: New optional field added to response

ground_truth.action_required: No action required. New optional metadata field in response.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Additive optional field should be low severity

llm_output.what_changed: Added a new optional field 'metadata' to the API response.
llm_output.action_required: Developers can now use the 'metadata' field if needed, but it's optional and can be ignored.

title: Multi-change diff: one breaking, one cosmetic

ground_truth.breaking_reason: Authentication method changed from query parameter to header. Also default limit changed from 50 to 25.
ground_truth.action_required: Update authentication to use Authorization header instead of query parameter. Also check pagination - default limit reduced from 50 to 25.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Multiple changes: breaking auth change + default value change + cosmetic whitespace. Should identify breaking one.

llm_output.what_changed: The authentication method has changed from query parameter to header-based authentication, and the default value for the 'limit' parameter was changed from 50 to 25.
llm_output.breaking_reason: Existing implementations that relied on query parameter authentication will no longer function, as this method has been removed.
llm_output.action_required: Update code to use header-based authentication by including the API key in the Authorization header as 'Authorization: Bearer xxx'. Also, adjust the default limit parameter to 25 if applicable.

title: Ambiguous parameter description rewording

ground_truth.action_required: Review timeout handling. The rewording suggests queries 'may be cancelled' (not 'will be terminated') and results aren't guaranteed. This could indicate a behavior change.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Ambiguous - could be just a wording clarification or could indicate a behavior change from guaranteed termination to possible cancellation

llm_output.what_changed: The description of the 'timeout' parameter was altered to clarify that results are not guaranteed after the timeout duration.
llm_output.action_required: Developers should review the new description to understand the implications of query timeouts.

title: Token endpoint now requires PKCE for public clients

ground_truth.breaking_reason: code_verifier becomes required. Public clients not using PKCE will get 400 errors.
ground_truth.action_required: Implement PKCE flow in all public clients. Add code_challenge to authorization requests and code_verifier to token requests. Deadline: April 1, 2026.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Clear breaking change with new required parameter

llm_output.what_changed: Public clients are now required to include PKCE parameters, specifically a 'code_verifier', when exchanging authorization codes.
llm_output.breaking_reason: Existing implementations that do not include a 'code_verifier' will start receiving 400 Bad Request errors from the API.
llm_output.action_required: Developers must update their implementations to include the 'code_verifier' parameter in requests to the /oauth/token endpoint.

title: Amazon S3 now requires virtual-hosted-style requests

ground_truth.breaking_reason: Path-style S3 requests will stop working. Applications using path-style URLs will receive 403 errors.
ground_truth.action_required: Migrate all S3 requests to virtual-hosted-style URLs. Update AWS SDK to latest version. Deadline: September 30, 2026.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Major breaking change with clear deadline

llm_output.what_changed: Path style requests have been deprecated and will be removed, requiring the use of virtual hosted style instead.
llm_output.breaking_reason: Existing code that uses path style requests will result in 403 Forbidden responses after the deprecation date.
llm_output.action_required: Developers must transition existing path style request implementations to virtual hosted style before September 30, 2026.

title: Lambda adds support for Node.js 22 runtime

ground_truth.action_required: Consider migrating to Node.js 22 runtime, especially since Node.js 20 support ends March 2026.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Additive new runtime option

llm_output.what_changed: Added new Node.js version (Node.js 22) to the support list in the documentation.
llm_output.action_required: No action required; simply be aware of the new supported Node.js version.

title: DynamoDB changes default read consistency for Global Tables

ground_truth.breaking_reason: ConsistentRead=true from non-home regions will throw ValidationException. Applications relying on cross-region strong consistency will break.
ground_truth.action_required: Audit Global Table reads. If using ConsistentRead=true from non-home regions, either route reads to home region or handle eventual consistency. Deadline: May 1, 2026.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Significant breaking change for distributed applications

llm_output.what_changed: The documentation clarifies that ConsistentRead=true is only supported for reads from the table's home region, and it specifies that using it from a non-home region will result in a ValidationException starting May 1, 2026.
llm_output.breaking_reason: Existing code that relies on strongly consistent reads from cross-region requests will fail and throw a ValidationException after the specified date.
llm_output.action_required: Developers need to modify their code to ensure that ConsistentRead=true is only used when reading from the home region of the table.

title: Dynamic Workers now in open beta

ground_truth.action_required: No action required. New feature available for dynamically creating Workers at runtime.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: New additive feature in beta

llm_output.what_changed: Introduced a new feature allowing Workers to create other Workers at runtime using the LOADER binding.
llm_output.action_required: Developers should review the new dynamic Workers feature and determine how it can enhance their existing implementations.

title: OIDC Claims filtering in Gateway policies

ground_truth.action_required: No action required. New OIDC Claims selector available for identity-based policies.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Additive new selector

llm_output.what_changed: An additional OIDC Claims row has been added to the API documentation.
llm_output.action_required: Review the updated documentation for the new OIDC Claims addition.

title: Containers now support Docker Hub images

ground_truth.action_required: No action required. Docker Hub images now available as alternative to Cloudflare Registry.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Additive feature, expands image source options

llm_output.what_changed: Containers now support images from both Cloudflare Registry and Docker Hub, not just Cloudflare Registry.
llm_output.action_required: No action required; developers can use images from Docker Hub in addition to Cloudflare Registry.

title: REST API: Deprecation of the team discussions API

ground_truth.action_required: Plan migration from Team Discussions API to GitHub Discussions before March 1, 2027 deadline.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Deprecation notice with clear removal date

llm_output.what_changed: The Team Discussions API has been marked as deprecated and will be removed on March 1, 2027.
llm_output.breaking_reason: The API will no longer be available after the deprecation date, requiring migration to a different API.
llm_output.action_required: Developers must migrate to GitHub Discussions before the API removal date.

title: New required permissions for GitHub App fine-grained tokens

ground_truth.breaking_reason: Permission requirement changed from read to write. Existing tokens with only read permission will fail.
ground_truth.action_required: Update fine-grained tokens to include contents:write permission before April 15, 2026.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Breaking permission change with deadline

llm_output.what_changed: The permission required for fine-grained tokens has changed from 'contents:read' to 'contents:write'.
llm_output.breaking_reason: Existing code that relies on 'contents:read' permissions will fail after the change as it will no longer have the required permissions for API access.
llm_output.action_required: Developers must update their permission scopes to include 'contents:write' instead of 'contents:read' in their API calls.

title: Code scanning API now returns severity field

ground_truth.action_required: No action required. New severity field available for enhanced filtering.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Additive field in response

llm_output.what_changed: The 'severity' field was added to both the root and rule objects in the API response.
llm_output.action_required: No action required as this is a non-breaking change.

title: Repository rename changes the API URL

ground_truth.breaking_reason: HTTP status code changed from 200 to 301. Clients not handling redirects will break.
affected_endpoints: All REST API endpoints using repository names
ground_truth.action_required: Ensure HTTP clients follow 301 redirects. Update any hardcoded repository URLs. Deadline: April 1, 2026.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Clear breaking change - HTTP status code change

llm_output.what_changed: API calls using the old repository name will now return a 301 Moved Permanently response instead of continuing to resolve to the new name automatically.
llm_output.breaking_reason: Existing code that relies on receiving a 200 OK response with data for the old name will fail to work correctly as it will now need to handle redirects.
llm_output.action_required: Developers must update their integrations to handle 301 redirects and follow the Location header to get the new repository URL.

title: Copilot usage metrics API now available

ground_truth.action_required: No action required. New endpoints available for Copilot usage tracking.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: New endpoints added, purely additive

llm_output.what_changed: Two new endpoints were added for fetching Copilot usage metrics and seat assignment details.
llm_output.action_required: No immediate action is required, but developers may want to review the new endpoints and integrate them if needed.

title: GraphQL API rate limit structure changed

ground_truth.breaking_reason: Rate limit headers changed. Code parsing old headers will break. Complex queries may hit limits sooner.
ground_truth.action_required: Update rate limit handling code to use new complexity-based headers. Review complex queries that may consume many points.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Breaking change in rate limit mechanism and headers

llm_output.what_changed: The rate limiting mechanism was changed from requests per hour/minute to complexity points per hour/minute, with new headers introduced to reflect this change.
llm_output.breaking_reason: Existing code that relies on the old request-count headers and rate limiting mechanism will no longer function correctly, as they are replaced by complexity points and new headers.
llm_output.action_required: Update code to handle complexity points for rate limiting and use the new headers X-RateLimit-Complexity-Remaining and X-RateLimit-Complexity-Reset.

title: Structured Outputs now support recursive schemas

ground_truth.action_required: No action required. Structured Outputs now support more complex schemas.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Relaxation of constraints, non-breaking

llm_output.what_changed: Increased maximum schema depth from 5 to 10 levels, added support for recursive types, and allowed `$ref` to self with recursion limit. Maximum properties per schema increased from 100 to 200.
llm_output.breaking_reason: Existing schemas that exceeded the previous limits or relied on the previous behavior of no recursion or self-referencing may not function as expected after the change.
llm_output.action_required: Review and update any schemas to accommodate the new limits and check for issues arising from the allowance of recursive types and self-references.

title: Transactions sync endpoint pagination change

ground_truth.breaking_reason: Page size reduced 5x. Code that assumes all transactions fit in one page may miss data.
ground_truth.action_required: Ensure pagination logic properly handles has_more flag and continues fetching. Test with accounts that have >100 recent transactions.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Subtle breaking change in pagination behavior

title: Removal of legacy v2 Mail Send endpoint

ground_truth.breaking_reason: v2 endpoint being removed entirely. All v2 users will get 410 Gone responses.
ground_truth.action_required: Migrate from v2 to v3 Mail Send endpoint before June 1, 2026.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Clear removal of deprecated endpoint

llm_output.what_changed: The v3 endpoint is now marked as 'Required' instead of 'Recommended', and the v2 endpoint has been marked as 'DEPRECATED - Removed June 1, 2026', indicating that it will no longer be supported after this date.
llm_output.breaking_reason: The v2 endpoint will be removed and is no longer usable; all users must transition to v3.
llm_output.action_required: Developers must migrate their implementations from the v2 endpoint to the v3 endpoint before June 1, 2026.

title: Adds Pay by Bank to the Billing API

llm_output.action_required: Developers using the API should review the new payment method and consider implementing it in their applications if applicable.

title: Makes Boleto tax ID nullable in payment method details for Payment Records

ground_truth.action_required: Review code that reads Boleto tax_id field - it can now be null. Add null checks if not already present.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Field changed from required to optional/nullable. Not breaking for request, but consumers parsing responses should handle null.

llm_output.what_changed: The 'tax_id' field's data type was changed from 'string (required)' to 'string or null', indicating that it can now accept a null value.
llm_output.breaking_reason: Existing code that expects 'tax_id' to always be a string will fail to handle cases where 'tax_id' is null.
llm_output.action_required: Developers must update their code to handle cases where 'tax_id' can be null, ensuring proper validation and null checks are implemented.

title: PostgREST default row limit changed from 1000 to 100

ground_truth.breaking_reason: Default row limit reduced 10x. Queries that relied on getting up to 1000 rows will now silently return fewer rows.
ground_truth.action_required: Add explicit .limit() to queries that need more than 100 rows, or increase db-max-rows in your project configuration.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Silent breaking change - queries return fewer rows without error

llm_output.what_changed: The default maximum number of rows returned by the API has been changed from 1000 to 100.
llm_output.breaking_reason: Existing queries expecting more than 100 rows will return fewer results, potentially causing application errors or mismatches.
llm_output.action_required: Developers need to either add explicit .limit() to their queries or update the db-max-rows configuration to accommodate the desired number of rows.

title: Unified Opt-Outs across RCS, SMS, and MMS channels

ground_truth.breaking_reason: Behavior change: opt-outs now cascade across channels. Messages that previously succeeded may now fail with Error 21610.
ground_truth.action_required: Review messaging logic. If you rely on channel-specific opt-outs, users who opt out of SMS will now also be blocked on RCS/MMS. Update opt-out recovery flows.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Significant behavior change, active by default

llm_output.what_changed: Opt-out management is now unified across SMS, MMS, and RCS channels, meaning opting out on one channel blocks all channels.
llm_output.breaking_reason: Existing implementation that relies on separate opt-out management per channel will no longer function correctly, as messages to opted-out users will be blocked across all channels.
llm_output.action_required: Review and update the opt-out handling logic to account for unified opt-out across all channels. Ensure you implement checks for the new error code 21610 when attempting to message opted-out users.

title: Increased MMS rate limits for A2P 10DLC Phone Numbers

ground_truth.action_required: Review your Brand Trust Score. High-trust brands benefit from higher limits. Low-trust brands retain 1 MPS.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Rate limit structure changed but default remains same for low-trust

llm_output.what_changed: The MMS rate limits have changed from a fixed rate of 1 MPS for all A2P 10DLC numbers to variable limits based on Brand Trust Score (10 MPS for High, 5 MPS for Medium, and 1 MPS for Low).
llm_output.breaking_reason: Existing implementations expecting a fixed rate limit of 1 MPS for all A2P 10DLC numbers will no longer function correctly as they need to handle multiple rate limits based on Brand Trust Score.
llm_output.action_required: Update the application logic to account for variable MMS rate limits based on the Brand Trust Score and campaign use case.

title: Certificate rotation for all REST API endpoints

ground_truth.breaking_reason: Certificate rotation will break clients that pin TLS certificates
ground_truth.action_required: If you pin certificates, update pinned certs before April 8, 2026. If you don't pin, no action needed.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Breaking only for certificate-pinning clients

llm_output.what_changed: A new TLS certificate will become effective on April 8, 2026, requiring updates to pinned certificates.
llm_output.breaking_reason: Existing integrations that pin certificates may fail to validate after the current certificate expires on April 8, 2026, if not updated.
llm_output.action_required: Update your pinned certificates to include the new certificate effective April 8, 2026, prior to that date.

title: Cipher suite list update for all REST API endpoints

ground_truth.breaking_reason: Legacy cipher suites being removed. Clients using only legacy ciphers will fail to connect.
ground_truth.action_required: Verify your TLS client supports modern cipher suites (TLS 1.3 AES-GCM or ChaCha20). Update before June 3, 2026.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Breaking for clients using only legacy ciphers

llm_output.what_changed: Removal of legacy TLS cipher suites from the supported list.
llm_output.breaking_reason: The removed cipher suites may still be in use by existing clients, which will cause them to fail to connect to the API after the effective date.
llm_output.action_required: Ensure your TLS client supports at least one of the retained cipher suites listed in the documentation.

llm_output.what_changed: Added new functionality for ConversationRelay, which connects voice calls to WebSocket servers and includes support for custom parameters and voice controls.

title: End-to-end encryption for Vercel Workflow

ground_truth.action_required: No action required. Data is now automatically encrypted. Previously restricted sensitive data can now be passed through Workflow.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Security improvement, no breaking changes

llm_output.what_changed: Workflow data, including inputs, step arguments, return values, hook payloads, and stream data, are now automatically encrypted end-to-end.
llm_output.action_required: No code changes required. It is now safe to pass sensitive data through Workflow steps.

ground_truth.action_required: No action required. New optional mode property available for setting file permissions.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Additive optional parameter

llm_output.what_changed: The API now allows setting file permissions directly using the optional `mode` property when writing files.
llm_output.action_required: Developers can now set file permissions directly in the API call; no immediate action is required unless they want to utilize this new feature.

title: Elastic build machines available in Beta

ground_truth.action_required: No action required. New elastic build machine option available in beta.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: New optional feature

llm_output.what_changed: The build machine options were changed from only Standard machines to include Fixed and Elastic machines, with Elastic being a beta feature that automatically assigns machines.
llm_output.breaking_reason: Existing projects may rely on the previous machine type specifications; depending on the project settings, code that assumes 'Standard' build machines may no longer function as expected with the new 'Fixed' and 'Elastic' options.
llm_output.action_required: Review and update project settings to specify the appropriate build machine type (Standard, Enhanced, Turbo, or Elastic).
